items.headline: Prenatal and early postnatal periods differentially shape the maturation of human cortical microstructure and myelin
items.preview: This study explores how prenatal and postnatal periods affect cortical development.
items.article:
During late gestation and early postnatal development, a combination of intrinsic and extrinsic factors drive the maturation of the human cortex. This process is regionally heterogeneous, with cortical areas developing at different paces and trajectories.
Why this matters: Understanding the distinct influences of prenatal and postnatal periods on cortical microstructure can provide insights into neurodevelopmental disorders and the biological underpinnings of cortical maturation.
The findings demonstrate that separating prenatal from postnatal influences offers finer insights into how human cortical myelin changes during perinatal development.

items.headline: TDP-43 is coming to the cottage: A new tool to study neurodegenerative diseases
items.preview: A new cell-based system captures TDP-43 aggregation and loss of function in neurodegenerative diseases.
items.article:
This study presents a novel cell-based system to model TDP-43 pathology, which is implicated in ALS and FTD. The system uses Förster Resonance Energy Transfer (FRET) to monitor TDP-43 aggregation and its nuclear loss of function.
Why this matters: This model allows researchers to study the mechanisms of TDP-43 aggregation and test potential therapies, providing a comprehensive tool to understand neurodegenerative diseases.
The findings reveal a cascade of events that lead to TDP-43 aggregation and subsequent loss of function, highlighting the interconnected nature of these processes.

items.headline: Put a ring on it: redesigning the delivery vehicles behind mRNA vaccines
items.preview: Researchers enhance mRNA vaccine delivery through modified lipid nanoparticles.
items.article:
Researchers at the University of Pennsylvania have developed modified lipid nanoparticles (LNPs) that improve mRNA vaccine delivery, reducing off-target effects and enhancing immune responses.
Why this matters: The ability to direct mRNA more precisely to lymph nodes could lead to lower doses needed for effective immunity, minimizing side effects associated with current formulations.
The new aroLNPs demonstrated strong immune potency and could pave the way for next-generation therapies targeting various diseases.

items.headline: Organoids: new Roche research centre in Basel will focus on alternative model systems
items.preview: Roche opens a new research centre focusing on organoids and alternative model systems.
items.article:
Roche has launched a new facility in Basel dedicated to human model systems, particularly organoids, to enhance drug discovery and reduce reliance on animal testing.
Why this matters: This initiative reflects a growing trend in the pharmaceutical industry to adopt organoid technologies, which could improve the predictive power of preclinical models and reduce costs in drug development.
The centre aims to foster collaboration between academia and industry, potentially leading to advancements in the use of organoids in medical research.

items.headline: Wave loses half its market cap on obesity data
items.preview: Wave Life Sciences faces market cap loss after disappointing obesity trial results.
items.article:
Wave Life Sciences reported disappointing results from its Phase I trial of WVE-007, leading to a significant drop in market capitalization. The trial data indicated no additional efficacy from higher doses of the siRNA compared to lower doses.
Why this matters: The results suggest that expectations for the drug's performance may have been overly optimistic, highlighting the challenges in developing effective obesity treatments.
The findings raise questions about the future of WVE-007 and its potential in the competitive obesity treatment market.
